title: Universal synchronous fifo ip core for field programmable gate arrays
abstract: A field programmable gate array (FPGA) device including a configuration interface arranged to receive configuration data from an FPGA programmer. The FPGA device includes a plurality of random access memory (RAM) types, including a first RAM type and a second RAM type, arranged to store the configuration or image data. The FPGA device also includes a FIFO IP core arranged to implement a FIFO function in a plurality of different FPGA platforms. The FIFO IP core is: i) configured to implement the FIFO in the FPGA device based on the configuration data, and ii) configurable to store the configuration data in one or both of the first RAM type and the second RAM type.
claims: Claims 1. A field programmable gate array (FPGA) device comprising: a configuration interface arranged to receive data from an FPGA programmer; a plurality of random access memory (RAM) types, including a first RAM type and a second RAM type, arranged to store the data; and a FIFO IP core arranged to implement a FIFO function in a plurality of different FPGA platforms, the FIFO IP core being: i) configured to implement the FIFO in the FPGA device based on the configuration data, and ii) configurable to store the data in one or both of the first RAM type and the second RAM type. 2. The device of claim 1 , wherein the FIFO IP core is configured to store the data in the at least one of the first RAM type and the second RAM type to balance the resource utilization. 3. The device of claim 2, wherein the FIFO IP core is configured to store the data in at least one of the first RAM type and the second RAM type to improve routing and timing. 4. The device of claim 1 , wherein the first RAM type includes block RAM and the second RAM type includes distributed RAM. 5. The device of claim 4, wherein the plurality of RAM types includes a third RAM type, the FIFO IP core being further configurable to store data in any combination of the first RAM type, the second RAM type, and the third RAM type, the third RAM type including UltraRAM. 6. The device of claim 1 , wherein the FPGA platform includes an FPGA type manufactured by one of XilinxÂ® corporation and IntelÂ® corporation. 7. The device of claim 6, wherein the FPGA types includes one of a model, family product and version the FPGA platform. 8. The device of claim 1 , wherein the FPGA development suite includes an IP catalog graphical user interface (GUI). 9. The device of claim 1 , wherein the configuration data is formed by Verilog hardware description language (FIDL). 10. The device of claim 9, wherein the Verilog FIDL is based on the IEEE 1364 standard. 11. A method for configuring an FPGA device implementing a universal FIFO IP core comprising: configuring the FIFO IP core to implement a FIFO function in the FPGA device; interfacing an FPGA programmer, via a communications interface, with the FPGA device; imaging the FPGA device with data arranged to implement the universal FIFO IP core in the FPGA device, the universal FIFO IP core being configurable to implement a FIFO function in a plurality of FPGA platforms; and storing the data in at least one of a first RAM type and a second RAM type in the FPGA device. 12. The method of claim 11 comprising storing the data in the at least one of the first RAM type and the second RAM type to balance the resource utilization. 13. The method of claim 12 comprising storing the data in at least one of the first RAM type and the second RAM type to improve routing and timing. 14. The method of claim 11 , wherein the first RAM type includes block RAM and the second RAM type includes distributed RAM. 15. The method of claim 14 comprising storing the image data in any combination of the first RAM type, the second RAM type, and a third RAM type, the third RAM type including UltraRAM. 16. The method of claim 11 comprising configuring the data with a synthesis directive to configure storage of the image data based on the FPGA platform. 17. The method of claim 11 , wherein the FPGA platform includes an FPGA type manufactured by one of XilinxÂ® corporation and IntelÂ® corporation. 18. The method of claim 17, wherein the FPGA types includes one of a model, family product and version the FPGA platform. 19. The method of claim 11 , wherein the FPGA development suite includes an IP catalog graphical user interface (GUI). 20. A non-transient computer readable medium containing program instructions for causing a computer to implement a universal FIFO IP core in an FPGA device comprising the method of: configuring the FIFO IP core to implement the FIFO function in the FPGA device based on the configuration data; interfacing with an FPGA programmer, via a communications interface, by the FPGA device; imaging the FPGA device with configuration data arranged to implement the universal FIFO IP core in the FPGA device, the universal FIFO IP core being configurable to implement a FIFO function in a plurality of FPGA platforms; and storing the image data in at least one of a first RAM type and a second RAM type in the FPGA device.
